{"step_by_step_thinking": "Selenocysteine is an amino acid that includes a post-translational modification, as it is inserted into proteins through a mechanism similar to that of stop codons. Triiodothyronine is a hormone, not an amino acid, and thus does not undergo post-translational modifications as amino acids do. Hydroxyproline and hydroxylysine are non-standard amino acids formed through post-translational modifications of proline and lysine, respectively. Therefore, the amino acid that does not include post-translational modification is Triiodothyronine.", "answer_choice": "B"}
